Table 1: Out-of-sample quantile score ratios relative to BVAR(12)

Figure 1: Brent oil prices, financial uncertainty, and distributional asymmetry: The left panel shows the evolution of log Brent oil prices and financial uncertainty over time. The two series are effectively uncorrelated, with a correlation coefficient of 0.02. The right panel plots financial uncertainty against a time-varying measure of skewness in log Brent prices. Financial uncertainty is negatively correlated with the third moment of log Brent prices (= −0.281). Time-varying skewness is computed using sample skewness over 48-month rolling windows. Financial uncertainty data are from Jurado et al. (2015).
